title: caryocaraceae
intro: The Caryocaraceae is a family of plants, specifically a group of flowering plants. It is a small family that consists of approximately 17 species, primarily found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The members of this family are commonly known as pequi trees or cupuacu trees, and are recognized by their unique, large fruit and showy flowers. The Caryocaraceae family is part of the larger group of angiosperms, characterized by the production of seeds within a fruit.
